text.title: w3c-design-tokens-standard Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for w3c-design-tokens-standard as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Migrating tokens by replacing `$ref` aliases with resolved hex values.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The alias graph is the primary value of the W3C format; a flattened file behaves identically to the legacy format being replaced.

failure-mode.description: Agent confuses W3C draft format with Tokens Studio JSON dialect.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both use `$type` but differ on composite type sub-schemas and alias syntax; explicitly specify the target format and version in every prompt.

failure-mode.description: Generated `$type` values use older names (e.g., `type` instead of `$type`).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The `$`-prefix is required by the draft; validators reject the unprefixed form. Enforce the `$`-prefixed form in a post-generation lint step.

failure-mode.description: Composite types (shadow, gradient, typography) generated with incorrect sub-schemas.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Composite type sub-schemas have evolved across draft versions; lint each composite against its specific sub-schema after generation.

failure-mode.description: Agent assumes Style Dictionary v4 syntax without checking the project version.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: v3 and v4 have incompatible transform APIs; pin the version explicitly in every agent prompt to avoid silent no-output bugs.
